channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day? — 39 replies — 156.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day?

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-05-26 17:44 UTC

OP

What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day?

Early replies (first 3)

62274691
>>62274690
Hedera CLPR just ate Chainlink's luch.

62274797
>>62274691
>zero iq take
Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass, which means theyre probably the ones going to implement it.

62274806
>>62274797
>Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass
Irrelevant. CLPR makes CCIP obsolete.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62274986
>>62274979
>do you know how fucking retarded you sound?
No I don't. Explain why I'm retarded with actual arguments. Or cope-

62274998
>>62274963
This will never happen without a big Mac being $75.

62275004
>>62274998
This lol

62275045
>>62274690
>Ass unlocked.
>Markets activated.

it's happening bros

62275104
>>62274963
There is no way. This would mean after 10 years we'd all have 9 figures there is no fucking way they'd let us make it that hard.

62275140
>>62274690
KABOOOOOOOOOOM

62275167
>>62274963
Fucking when
I’d be ecstatic to have $0.01 per LINK per day at this point

62275273
>>62275104

Low 7 figs per year is not some magical unattainable # in the future American economy.

System is rigged bias is the dumbest low IQ take in history.

System is incompetent, slow and inefficient. But eventually gets to the stable value.

62275301
>>62274797
>Theyre on the Hedera council

anyone else find it hilarious all these grand titles crypto scammers give themselves

one week they're poker players, failed salesmen, etc., then they join a crypto company as an executive board member. then their gullible investors give themselves battle names (army, rangers, marines, etc)

reminds me of the larpers from role models

62275343
>>62275167
that's not realistic, HOWEVER you will have LINK at $0.01 eventually.

62275385
>>62274986
>>62274841
>>62274806
>>62274691
There's a reason CLPR is only live on Hedera itself; it's impossible to implement on other chains without either incurring massive gas costs, or compromising on data security by gimping the system.

CLPR works like Cosmos or Avalanche in that there's a registry of validators. Keeping this registry alive on a foreign chain requires continuous state tracking. This means massive amounts of gas.
Bypassing this gas cost involves cutting corners. Massive ones.

62275388
>>62274690
those banners go for about $300 at fedex office.

62275410
>>62274690
>>62274848
how would an asset worth $9 pay out $1 per day? where does the money come from?

62275479
>>62275410
you are talking to iq80 retards who also yolo'd their meager savings and have no psychological way out besides suicide or delusions.

62275500
>>62275410
Staking returns aren't proportional to the Link price, they're proportional to usage fees which are measured in USD.

62275555
>>62274690
Sounds great when does this start fellow marine?

62275595
>>62274690
>Stinkies really thought some stinky russian made a magical piggie bank that would give them returns beyond their wildest dreams

62275635
>>62275555
Two weeks

Impressive digits btw

62275645
>>62274690
chainlink staking is economic security and the incentive is to pay $1 per link per day? good! KABOOM

62275660
>>62274690
What do you not understand about crypto being dead, Web3 never happening, and Chainlink’s “use case” being obsolete?

62275685
no I meant the price of chainlink will be equivalent to $1for every day of the week
I'm not talking about rewards or anything else
the price of chainlink will be $7

62275738
>>62275660
... and then you woke up and realized DTCC is going into production with Chainlink later this year.

62275779
>>62275385
you said that in another thread, whered you get this info? post sauce

62275789
>>62275738
Oh wow, two years of insiders trading at low volume before people realize tokenized RWA are stupid because why even do that when there’s no chance of outsized returns and doing fraud which were unironically crypto’s biggest selling points?

62275818
>>62275779
>you said that in another thread
I definitely did not, I only researched this myself today.

This is all public information, Hedera, Cosmos, Avalanche have it all laid out in their documentation.

>>62275789
just look at this incoherent seethebabble

62275885
>>62275385
>it's impossible to implement on other chains
This is not what they are saying, just check the hedera sub, it's looking rather grim for ccip

62275919
>>62275818
So no actual counterargument? All you have to do is show me any evidence that DTCC tokenized assets are generating ANY hype right now.

62275933
>>62274690
Ok. But.... price?? Oh.. and chart???

62275992
>>62275933
going up

62276072
>>62275789
lolwut

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 35 replies — 140.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-05-26 17:35 UTC

OP

KEVIN WARSH: WE ARE GOING TO POP THE HOUSING MARKET BUBBLE

Early replies (first 3)

62269301
>>62269297
Oh thank God!

62269302
Won’t do shit
Trump won’t allow it

62269305
Can this nigga wait until btc dumps to $30k plsss

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269319
>>62269309
Deporting 100 million would also solve housing.

62269321
>>62269297
imagine taking out a 100k 5x leveraged investment and it drops 20k in value while index funds go up like 40%

62269323
>>62269319
Anything but solving the real issue. Kick the can down the road enough and you'll go too far to turn back before you know it.

62269325
>>62269319
They've started going after financial institutions who service illegals and will probably start going after other institutions too in order to force illegals to self-deport. So it appears that they're doing both

62269418
>>62269297
So?

62269424
>>62269309
>KEVIN WARSH: WE ARE GOING TO POP THE HOUSING MARKET BUBBLE
Lowing rates increases the price of housing, trump with his real estate assets said he won't let pricing come down.

62269437
is it over?

62269442
>>62269319
Why not 200 million?

62269443
>>62269301
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ce-LjCpserA
Perfect time for a gif of this.

62269468
>>62269424
Trump is cooked after midterms. Lame duck

62269473
>>62269437
nah just keep buying

62269536
>>62269473
It that my nigga palmbeach? Haven't seen that nigga since the Boca bash in 2018.

62269553
>>62269468
More like a lame cuck desu

62269564
>>62269297
>this guy
>popping anything more than a pimple on his ass
pick 1 and only 1

62269581
>>62269442
because veggies won’t pick up themselves

62269586
>>62269309
>shrink the Fed's bloated balance sheet—including selling off its roughly \(\$2\) trillion in mortgage-backed securities—
so, short the shit out of the stock market?

>cutting short-term interest rates.
wtf?

>>62269424
>>62269468
they really want to tank the value of the dollar lmao

>\(\$2\)
bot?

62269589
>>62269309
>pandemic-era asset bubbles
Is it finally time to short GME to zero?

62269600
>>62269319
Jews won't allow it

>>62269325
Retard

62270026
>>62269536
you know it is, playa!

62270033
Interest rates go down prices go up. Interest rates go up prices go up. What do you all not understand?

62270069
>>62270033
chekt, rekt and kekt

62271393
>>62269424
>believing anything Trump says
Lmao. The wall just got 3 ft higher, right?

62273816
>>62271393
Yes, and mexico paid for it.

62275402
>>62273816
How much has Mexico paid in tarifs?

62275863
>>62269309
That's a bold move, Cotton. Let's see if it pays off

62275928
>>62269319
We should keep all illegals. Everyone in our borders can stay if they want.
But we kill the blackest 1% of the country every year. All jews removed from earth, preferably relocated to the sun, live streamed of course.

62275975
The writing has been on the wall for a while, the elites just constantly go bearish and dump a certain asset so they can load up again. Normies thought property was safe, bought boomer houses at the peak, now it's time to take their money. Simple as.

62275991
>>62269297
HE BETTER NOT MOTHER FUCKER THIS IS MY 'MERICUA INVESTMENT

62276016
>>62269319
no it wouldn't. it's a problem of excess credit availability.

62276036
>>62269297
Been hearing this for the last 15 fucking years wake me up when something actually happens

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-05-26 17:37 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272848
>>62272812
Why are you brown?

62272849
>>62272835
No but very tough to prove or prosecute

62272858
>>62272835
You don't get to complain about this after Biden. Sorry. Thats just how it works.

62272965
>>62272848
I'm not brown, I'm Irish-American

62273031
>>62272812
gg Anon

62273035
>>62272848
why do white niggers assume everyone who went to college or grad school is brown or a chink? should have paid attention in class, dumbfuck.

62273041
>>62272965
the irish people have done nothing for humanity, and neither will you. You are niggers compared to Russians. Afrikkkan niggers have accomplished more.

62273047
do they teach all law students how to act in court, even if you don’t become the kind of lawyer who has to go to court?

62273090
>>62273047
First year was very basic, we had one oral argument in our legal writing class and that was it. Other than that all classes were just like normal undergrad classes, just law related. There is optional stuff like a Mock Trial tournament every year but they don't make people do that.

62273152
>>62272812
>AMA law related
How much upwards mobility do you think a law degree gives someone? I heard that the rich kids are setup by their parents with the chancery and all while the poor kids struggle to make mich out of their degree.

62273154
>>62272812
what will happen with chainlink on 6/6/26?

62273163
>>62273152
Well I haven't graduated yet, but employment data for my school is really good. It all depends on where you go and how well you do it seems.
>>62273154
It will skyrocket from $9.44 to $9.45

62273171
does it seem like most of your classmates are smart? I was at a high school reunion recently and spoke to a guy who loafed around doing menial work for like 15 years after college. then he took the lsat and got into Northwestern. he has a big time job now defending people in trouble with the SEC.

62273174
>>62273171
Yeah, how many law students are just dumbfucks?

62273234
Junior lawyer is literally technician in legal system. Very far from management and get treated like disposable human trash

62273383
>>62272812
be like this guy anon
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G-6eynsaZ0U&t=836s

62273401
>>62272812
Should I become a lawyer? My college is paid for and I feel like it'll be easy with AI now

62273402
>>62273041
The Irish are better writers the British

62273648
>>62273171
>>62273174
Nobody I'd call dumb but some came across as pretty lazy. Also AI use is absurd.
>>62273401
It's worth it as long as you don't go to a predatory school and don't drown in debt. What really matters is your LSAT and undergrad GPA. You can use this site to put in your undergrad GPA and see what different LSAT scores will get you into and how much in scholarships. It's mostly LSAT though that determines it, but there's a lot of free LSAT test prep stuff online and it is a very learnable test.
https://lsd.law/law-school-chances-predictor

62273654
Lawyers will be replaced with A.I.

62275142
>>62273654
Sort of like how your dad was replaced by my penis

62275176
>>62272812
Tits or ass

62275435
>>62272812

Are you going to be an actual trial attorney with balls or be a paper pushing cuck like most?

I can't make up my mind who's intelligence the general public overrates more between doctors and lawyers.

62275484
>>62272812
What was your LSAT score? What is your school’s rank? What was your GPA? Where are you interning this summer?

How big is your pp?

62275816
>>62275176
Ass for most of my life but as I’ve gotten older, tits
>>62275435
I honestly don’t know yet, it’s only been one year and I’m not even dead set on which area of the law I want to practice in.
>>62275484
>What was your LSAT score?
170
>What is your school’s rank?
Mid 20s
>What was your GPA?
In undergrad or 1L? In undergrad it was a 3.4 in an easy major, I just didn’t give a shit in college. In 1L it was a 3.5
>Where are you interning this summer?
I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

>How big is your pp?
5.8 inches massive

62275842
Do you ever consider yourself a second class profession compared to finance chads who value and trade and make money off the silly little financial obligation contracts lawyers write up?

62275952
>>62275842
Again dude I just finished my first out of three years of law school, so I don’t know what the actual practice looks like at all.

62276043
>>62275952
I'm just ribbing you buddy, a little white collar banter if you will

item.title: /Biz revival — 117 replies — 92.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 117 • Updated: 2026-05-26 16:29 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62267369
>>62265467
greetings anon i have just returned from a hiatus therefore the board is alive again. the only coin you need now is $PUMP. the irony is $PUMP killed crypto by making it possible to launch so many coins that there became no community like there used to be, so now we have to invert that into wealth by buying PUMP. you'll see. bull run hasn't even begun.

62267402
Every single factor that led to GME and CAR going parabolic is perfectly aligned for GRPN in 6 days.

62267687
>>62265467
I regret selling my litecoin and I've bought some of it back. It has a strong narrative as OG coin. There's the livVM being worked on which will bring eth style smart contracts to it. IDK how decentralized it will be but it's a start.

ICP. Do yourself a favor and actually read some of the tech details of how it works. They're setting up "sovereign networks" signing deals with heads of state. Prepping for the future. Chainkey cryptography is wizardry.
The biggest downside, IMO, is the UI is pretty shit - you have to sign a lot of "canisters" (smart contracts) to get going. But it's the only tech that combines blockchain with website plus is reasonably distributed.

62269707
>>62267687
litecoin has developped MWEB network, which is basically private transactions integrated into the already ltc blockchain. it's a pretty big deal, like an integrated coin mixer that is fully private (can't decipher it just like monero).
Very few apps use it yet.
Once it finally gets some sufficient support on any ltc wallet, it will be much like zcash. You go into MWEB, and every public trace stop there. you can also go out of mweb of course.

62269750
Is everyone here stuck in 2017? How about a coin that has come out in the past half decade gramps.

62270867
Ntdoy and rare earth etfs. Some uranium too (nuclear nrgy not nukes)

62270942
I find it so hard to browse this board these days, the humor is so so tiring. Just the same jokes over and over again. I know some people say they find gems here every know and then but I never have, anyone got any advice? I’m so tired man

62271126
>>62265467
Why buy LITS instead of LTC?

62271298
>>62271126
because it's like buying litecoin with a discount, and when ltc pumps LITS will pump multiple X more just like micro strategy did with btc

62271371
>>62265467
>Ton
Turns out that's in my bookmarks as something with potential. I looked at Litecoin when I first got into crypto, but I figured these long-standing projects like it, LINK, HBAR, etc. were things I'd come back to later with gains from lower market cap stuff. Seems that was the correct decision since so many of them are now around the same price as they day I bought my first assets.
I figured they might make great strides someday but not in a hurry since they'd already make great strides (price-wise) since their debuts. Despite that I'm shocked to see Litecoin is like half the price of when I first looked at it.
I'd recommend some stuff myself but I'm still in that newish area where I've made good picks after reading up on stuff before buying (the DYOR part /biz/ always told me to do) but couldn't briefly articulate why someone should take a look at something at current prices. Everything that's done me well is more expensive now than when I first bought it.

62271377
>>62265877
I don't think there's a LUNC thread up now but I remember seeing one recently where there was a lot of reasonable optimism.

62271378
>>62265467
It's dead Jim

62271387
>>62270867
What's the basis for uranium at this time? I saw a few months ago people talking about various reactors, from new mini ones to restarting some existing ones.

62271407
>>62271387
an anon shilled some us stock, nano nuclear energy and i didn't buy because the chart looked already pumped. it went down a lot since like 40%

Nano nuclear energy. I looked it up they're trying to develop mini nuclear reactors thant can be moved around, like for data centers etc. But i'm wondering isn't that tech super risky?in any case that might or might not be a great moonshot. No idea, don't own any but looking at it

62271421
>>62271407
I can see what you would be. I remember seeing some airplane-lifted reactors some months ago. Operation Windlord. Valar Atomics, though, they are not publicly traded.
I see what you mean about NNE having doing about a 10x since it began and now being like 50% down since then. Still, nice to have specific leads on what to research.

62271501
>>62265467
Its dead because there havent been enough nigger days due to the bull market

62271653
How about starting with removing this faggot nigger captcha and having to wait for a minute for it? I gave up posting here, because of it, honestly. And I'm not verifying my email each time either.

62271675
>>62271653
Captchas become faster and easier if you post more

62271760
>>62271675
and then reset your progress for no reason
no, I didn't erase my cookies, just couldn't post for TWO DAYS

62271767
>>62271760
yes jannies trannies destroyed it all. it's over. At least biz is the only white board left

62271790
>>62265467
good pick with litecoin anon, literally the only coin which can reliably process frequent micropayments without clogging or taking too many fees


it will be the coin of choice for autonomous AI agents to transact with eachother in the future

62271899
>>62265467
what killed this place to begin with is that there is no business or finance talk going on here. Its just retarded "buy this crypto coin!!!"

62272124
>>62265467
>LTC
Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.

>TON
Shit is never going to pump.

>UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.
>IF
They won't ever back down their bullshit, even in the deepest shit they still prefered to appeal to niggers and fags instead of making games.

62272342
>>62272124
>Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.
they just developped MWEB transactions anon. Try harder.

why wouldn't ton pump? are you retarded?

nvgmi

62272366
>>62265467
india and china need to be banned from this board

62273281
>>62271298
Seems like it crashes X times more too unfortunately. Maybe a good idea for ltc exposure but I’m really unsure if litecoin will be the play next bull

62273290
>>62265467
>we need to revive biz
>how do we do it you ask
>well its simple you just buy my decade old bags of a shitcoin so i can finally break even
well guess we found aid devastated bald whiny fag and its op

62274410
>>62269707
Yeah I think Litecoin "trojan horses" privacy into mainstream cryptocurrency. The marketing around it has always been "OG coin".
Eventually the markets will realize privacy is useful for more than cipherpunkery, and is embraced by big business.
Related, there was a lot of ZK research done by Polygon teams, but their funding ran out, I think? There's some other smaller projects experimenting with very compact proofs, Mina Protocol I think. I haven't researched that much. I'm a fan of LTC for being OG - strong codebase.

62274471
>>62273281
crashes x times more

not really. right now the price of the stock is like a 20% discount over their litecoin treasury. it gets giga hyped if litecoin moons. Now if litecoin were to moon for real one day (let's say 800$) this stock would probably x 50 from where it is.
The thing i don't like about LITS is basically not your key, not your cryptos. So i'm 30% LITS 70% litecoin

>>62274410
it's really a revolution. I think btc will implement the same thing. It's an integrated coin mixer that can't be traced basically.

62275749
>>62272342
few people know that aside from the privacy aspect, mweb also makes the network scalable because of pruning

without pruning a blockchain ends up taking infinite storage space

item.title: How is this still at #2? — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is this still at #2?

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-26 17:40 UTC

OP

Inspite of all FUD, and poor performance, somehow it has managed to stay at #2

Early replies (first 3)

62273485
It wont exist soon. Crypto is a failed industry as a whole. It's dead.

62273495
>#2
You've lost over 10% if you held ETH for the last 5 years. Being #2 just shows how garbage all of crypto is.

62273649
>>62273445
Only coin needed.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273668
Because GCR said it’s going to 10k one day

62273669
If I hadn't already made it a couple of years ago, I would still be dumping thousands into ETH every month. It's deflationary, the token of choice for RWA tokenization, getting adopted by institutional, etc. Highly highly likely that it grows to $10k+ over the next few years.
>oh no only a 5x from here???
Ok show me a better asset

62273675
>>62273669
I sold all 12 of my ETH, last of it at 2400. I’ll just start DCA’ing once it dumps below 1800 in the coming days/weeks/months

62273682
>>62273669
>>62273675

IMO best to buy it only if it bottoms on the ETHBTC pair. Was the only point where you made some money with ETH this cycle.

62273683
>>62273669
Its not deflationary atm.

62273689
>>62273682
I hear what you’re saying, and I should probably be more knowledgeable with analysis, but how about you just spoonfeed me the number I should be looking for on that chart. 0.23 for recent years bottom or further back and 0.21 or lower?

62273755
>>62273689
Somewhere around there. I think it’s a good idea in general to look at alts against btc, if btc is outperforming your coin you’re better off just holding btc. It’s zoomed out a lot here but that bounce off of that bottom really was the only time where you could make some good money compared to holding btc this cycle. (Also probably the only altcoin trade that I made money on this cycle desu)

62273776
>>62273669
you asked for a better asset. Zcash is becoming the Schelling point for non-DNM privacy (which is 100x bigger TAM than "we love criminals and hate the jooooz" Monero).
with Grayscale ETF (filed on May 12) it is basically over.

62273780
>>62273776
Scam

62273782
>>62273776
Jewish scam.

62273822
>>62273675
Fuck, I should have done that

62275634
Don't get all the Eth hate. Eth is awesome and I use it all the time for DeFi.

62275683
>>62273495
good thing I've held eth for longer than 5 years

62275776
>>62275683
>Luna is the best crypto to ever exist
>who cares that it rugged it and it was classified as a scam
>it gave me the biggest gain % ever more than bitcoin and eth combined and thats why its the best and number 1

62275794
>>62273669
>eth to 10k in 2021 its preprogrammed chuds
>erhm we meant 10k in 2025 heh
>next few years for sure trust pedoskelly
literally better chances of gambling away your funds in shitcoins kek and i say this as an eth ico buyer

62275834
>>62273445
because it is the smart contract blockchain, just like bitcoin is the crypto currency

62276051
>>62273445
What's crazier is XRP at #4 still sitting down more than 50% off a high it set in 2017

62276058
If Bitcoin runs again this year, ETH will get flipped by XRP, BNB and maybe even Solana.

item.title: /BIZ/ YLYL — 58 replies — 67.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BIZ/ YLYL

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-05-26 16:53 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272148
>>62271628
top tier retarded

62272176
>>62272167
lost again

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-26 17:43 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62276007
>>62275930
Yes it is in every thread right now becase I am posting about it nigger, because I hold LINK and find it strange how everyone is just fucking ignoring it ok? How about you discuss the tech instead of making up stories about hedera shills.

62276018
>>62276007
nobody is ignoring it 'nigger' (oh wow you're clearly from here so impressed), nobody's making up stories if you've been on this board for years you'd know, the fact you chose to get offended about that says it all though. also "discuss the tech" kek there's nothing to discuss, if there was you and the hbar shills would be doing it. you're just spamming buzzword and pretending to be fearful uncertain doubtful link baggies.

62276041
>>62276018
wordswordswords yet you said nothing regarding CLPR, only adhoms directed at me, because you did zero fucking research on this CLPR thing and are just complacent that whatever this new tech is it won't kill CCIP.

62276069
>>62276041

Im sure DTCC / SWIFT / euroclear are willing to switch to this brand new wonder technology after working with Chainlink for +10 years

item.title: I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino — 23 replies — 16.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-26 16:23 UTC

OP

I have 15 in total now.. I am gonna hodl them.

Early replies (first 3)

62270209
>>62270028
Swap for XMR and hodl

62270220
>>62270209
Thats my plan but Monero's price is just too high right now. So, I will just wait and see for now. LTC is near its this years low, so it makes sense holding it or even buying more of it.

62271797
aim for 50 at least, this coin is going to power the decentralized AI agent economy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271814
sooooo who is going to tell him?

62272140
>>62270028
I have 800

62272349
no one mentions MWEB which is one solid reason to be bullish on LTC

62272353
>>62272349
qrd?
Also what is the make it and sui stack for this?

62272539
>>62272353
It's a lightweight privacy extension, essentially. It hides the amount and receiver. It doesn't hide the sender though (though there are some tools like Hector Chu's coinjoin that would essentially do this if people used it.) Make it stack on LTC has always been 84. Same as having 21 BTC since it has a maximum supply of 84 million.

As for SUI, be very careful of VC coins people shill. Lots of insiders staking the premine waiting to dump it. I'm not saying don't buy it, just be aware of that. There will come a day when people that got it for free (including people marketing it, which is probably how you heard about it someone marketing it getting paid in the coin and not disclosing they got a shitload of them for free). Not saying they can't pump, but they can dump very hard and never recover. Fun to swing trade though.

62272587
>>62272539
How many LTC or XMR do you have sensei?

62272618
>>62272587
I stopped buying XMR when it went to $300. It could potentially be the most valuable coin, but it has so many liquidity channels cut off I'm happy with where I'm at. You should probably treat the make it stack as 21.

Both the qubic event in XMR and inflation bug in LTC (even though it was caught and didn't do permanent damage) have made me weary on privacy coins. I think you should have them, but BTC should be your core stack if you're new. If I were just entering the market, I'd probably pick up an LTC stack first since it's so cheap right now, then an XMR stack. Not necessarily a make it stack, but just something so you don't FOMO if they pump. Since BTC.d is so high, it'd pick up the alts for maybe a year (just so you don't fomo them later) and then never buy them again and focus on accumulating BTC at any price. I might be wrong and BTC just accelerates higher, but I don't think it'll go so high so fast it'll make much of a difference if you pick up the alts first. 1st generation PoW is pretty ignored right now except BTC, so they are probably safe bets. Of course I'll keep my on stack amounts a secret. Nothing good can come from telling anyone.

62272639
>>62272618
Thanks, I appreciate the advice :)

62272654
>>62272618
do you think xmr could realistically do a huge X from where it is?

62272668
>>62272654
Yeah. BTC ran to 20k in 2017 on retail fomo essentially. There has been a huge increase in the global money supply since then. Honestly, BTC could go to like 700k and it wouldn't surprise me, XMR could go to 4k and it wouldn't surprise me and LTC could go to $800 and it wouldn't surprise me. All this shit is undervalued. Another thing is fiat always dies. All fiat currencies have died historically and the governments of the world are doing the same thing that has killed all fiat currencies historically. It's just a matter of when. In the mean time though, we still have to live in a world where we settle in fiat, so don't invest more than you can afford to lose. Just treat these things as extra savings accounts.

62272838
>>62271797
BULLSHIT

62272845
>>62272539
No one said a fucking thing about sui retarded nigger he asked what a suicide stack was.

62272863
>>62271814
Who cares? Swap vs keep. It’s still a lose-lose bet in all altcoins atp, even Ai agrees lmao

62272971
>>62272845
It specifically says sui you dumb nigger. Not that guys fault you guys speak in nigger babble slang rather than typing it out.

62273235
>>62272863
Yeah Bitcoin is the only way to not loose money, why even have a bank account just keep all your life savings in bitcoin, since it's so stable and basically a guaranteed profite. You go first anon.

62273441
>>62270220
>Monero's price is just too high right now
Sub 300 is gone forever

62273491
>>62272971
you must be over 18 to post on this site

>>62273235
everything’s fake and gay anyway

62273626
>>62273235
Bitcoin is the only crypto club where there’s a long waiting list to get in and you’ll never get turned away because you didn’t follow the dress code that only changed because your ugly ass showed up or even if the club is filled to capacity

62275417
>>62273626
The fuck are you even trying to say?

62275720
>>62272838
enjoy being poor and destitute when the economy is completely overtaken by AI systems and you are relegated to the permanent bottom feeder caste

item.title: What happens to profit margins of US companies when the US government runs out of rugs to pull out from under Chinese co… — 9 replies — 9.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
What happens to profit margins of US companies when the US government runs out of rugs to pull out from under Chinese co…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-26 16:48 UTC

OP

What happens to profit margins of US companies when the US government runs out of rugs to pull out from under Chinese competitors? Eventually, China is going to become fully self-sufficient in turbofans, microchip manufacturing equipment, etc.

Early replies (first 3)

62275081
>>62274978
My dear child, China just learned how to make the tiny ball bearings for ballpoint pens about five years ago. Most of "their" tech is IP they've stolen from companies that were forced by their investors to set up manufacturing in China because "it's so cheap, bro!" China still cannot make modern jet engines; their best effort is a 1960s era Rolls-Royce engine copy that Rolls-Royce inexplicably sold them a complete manufacturing license and all the tech for. (Read Stanley Hooker's biography "Not Much of an Engineer" and you have to wonder which side the British were on during the Cold War.)

By the time China catches up to where we are today, we'll have cities on Mars.

62275193
>>62274978
>ITS DA CATACLYSM INCOMING
>ITS DA HYPERINFLASHUN
>ITS DA BANK RUN

kys

62275259
>>62275081
You sound like a person who has been in a coma since 2006.

You're talking about the Rolls Royce Spey as China's best turbofan, as if that was up-to-date info. Leaving aside the matter of low-bypass turbofans, since that isn't relevant to the airliner industry, China's best high-bypass turbofan in production right now is the WS-20, which is the relatively conservative design with a bypass ratio of 6 and so is basically on par with 1970s tech. The more more ambitious CJ-1000A is in flight testing right now and is supposed to be ready by 2028. It will probably be delayed, but once it is ready, the C919 will have a competitive engine that can't be export controlled. The engine is the most difficult part to substitute and so is the main rugpull threat holding back investment into expansion of C919 production lines right now

What is the point you wanted to make by highlighting ballpoint pen bearings? It's a highly niche industry, with relatively little money involved. It sounds to me like you're saying that, some years ago, the Chinese became so good at the technology relevant to ballpoint pen bearings that it became feasible for them to start to compete in the market with the industry leaders abroad? Was that meant as an illustration of how the Chinese are seemingly gunning for every industry, no matter how small or niche it might be?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275278
>>62275193
I'm not saying Nvidia or Micron profit margins going to be crushed tomorrow. The China threat to profits might be up to 15 years away for some industries.

62275296
>>62275278
nothing ever happens. Just don't buy their shit, worst case scenario

62275320
>>62275296
The US might use tariffs and bans to ward of the Chinese competition, but that won't save US companies' profits from sales in foreign markets

62275334
>>62275193
well bonds don't look good for the us.

62275374
>>62274978
>Eventually, China is going to become fully self-sufficient in turbofans, microchip manufacturing equipment, etc.
That’s kind of the crux - the only reason they’re behind in those industries is because of the manufacturing secret sauce needed for advanced designs. Otherwise, their engineers are smarter, work harder, and there’s a fuck ton of them; they out design and out engineer western companies already. See the automotive industry - Chinese companies have leapfrogged everyone to the point where the fucking CEO of Ford drives a Xiaomi (kek). Even if you don’t believe that Chinese engineers are better, just remember that western companies have engineering offices in China (like NVIDIA) which makes copying designs stupid easy. Anyways, it’s not a matter of if but when. If you work at a university/have friends that do, it’s quite obvious that our education system has failed and we’re graduating retards compared to the Chinese. The west’s dominance in technology is soon to be over, sadly.
>t. engineering consultant

62275841
>>62275374
So, what does that mean for stock valuations over the long term?

62275372

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275384
>>62275303
but anon there are jobs. just not jobs in working with your brain. the white collar class is finished.
i'm going back to customer service, nice a comfy. no expectations, no demand. show up, be polite, don't call anyone a racial slur. get paid. could not be easier.

62275395
>>62275303

62275396
>>62275384
> no racial slurs
how is this possible?

62275485
>>62275372
FP&A since I thought there was an abundance of jobs.

62275493
>>62275303
nig ger

62275519
>>62275303
Don't underestimate the power of positive thinking.

Close your eyes and say "I will get a job at JPMorgan" 5 times every morning and evening, and you will manifest it into reality.

62275548
>>62275519
I will get hired at JPMorgan so I can get dommy mommied by a director

62275619
>>62275485
How does FP&A work if you're only trained in finance and not in the underlying business

62275630
>>62275303
>A FINANCE DEGREE
>JOBS
what job exactly did you expect to get?
nobody wants a stupid teenager to tell them about finance
go work at mcdonalds or amazon

62275716
>>62275303
Sorry anon, you don't have the experience to qualify for this entry level role

item.title: >If governments can just print money out of thin air, why do we pay taxes? — 12 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>If governments can just print money out of thin air, why do we pay taxes?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-26 16:15 UTC

OP

>If governments can just print money out of thin air, why do we pay taxes?
>If Inflation is just a hidden tax, why not just "tax" us by printing money instead of requiring an itemized receipt
Oh my that's MMT! Very unpatriotic! Stop noticing! Don't you know that MMT is deboonked? CNBC says so!

Early replies (first 3)

62272395
>>62272334
it has to be as convoluted, inefficient, and suffering-inducing as possible

62272410
I'm an asset holder so I don't really care to argue this. Inflationary policy only benefits me. Maybe in the past I'd take the bait and argue but now, go ahead and print ad infinitum

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272415
>>62272410
>already print money and give most of it away to asset holders
>they pay a tiny portion back as taxes
how about we cut out the middleman and just print way less while achieving the same goal?

62272427
>>62272334
#1. The US Government doesn't print money, 97% of all dollars in existence have been created by commercial banks making loans.

#2. The Federal Reserve also doesn't print money. It just creates bank reserves which stay between the banks for overnight settlements and has nothing to do with regular people or regular businesses.

#3. Paying taxes actually does lower/reduce CPI, because it's pulling liquid cash away from the people who immediately spend it on consumer goods. If every poor and middle-class American suddenly had an extra $1,000/mo in their paychecks they would immediately go blow it on new cars, new homes, new furniture, upgrades on their real estate, eating out at restaurants and going on vacations. You'd have a spike in demand for low-end consumables without the extra productivity from people working more. Covid should've already taught you what happens when people are working way less while getting free-gibs in the mail.

#4. If you want to fix the inflation problem, you need to incentivize banks to make loans directed towards the production of goods, services and entrepreneurship. While disincentivizing banks from making loans towards asset purchases and consumption, like stocks, real estate, credit cards, etc.

Richard Werner explained all of this in detail years ago.

62272429
>>62272415
Yea sure but then you have to cut spending too. It's a hard sell and I don't think the masses are ready for that.

62272449
>>62272334
Keeping your citizens distracted, broke, and bogged down by regulations/laws is important if you are running a government.

62272468
You pay taxes and the US only prints so much fiat so entitys continue to buy our bonds so bread only costs $2 a loaf instead of $30

62272471
>>62272334
We all gonna be rich, the computers printing a Golden swan 6-9 months with Asia unstoppable

62272489
>>62272334
The govt doesn’t do this. They have the printers but have to ask the fed permission first to borrow against our tax dollars which they borrow from us interest free. That’s why they made trump prez to clean house and made it impossible for the dems by pitting him up against kamala instead of bernie or some shit since the dem politicians were borrowing against their own salaries with our tax money. The banks are ironically innocent this time. The 2008 housing bubble recession was very embarrassing for us on a global scale. They vowed never to allow it to happen again after all that bail out cash was eventually paid back

62272499
Taxes don't fund the government. They balance citizen accounts issued by the IMF, BIS, and World Bank. Any surplus, which is really a deficit in our debt-based financial system, goes to the British monarchy.

62275681
>>62272334
The inflation tax doesn't raise enough money

You will be priced out. They will not sell. I will read my archive of Bottomtober posts and smile until my face is sore.

62272560

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 260 replies — 52.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 260 • Updated: 2026-05-26 12:47 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265896
>>62264378
>This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.
the stemfag market was goated 12 years ago, now you have to be the throte goat to find anything

62265903
>>62265888
My gf keeps eating the cum before it reaches her pussy though.

62265928
>>62265903
Wrong hole.
Blowjobs are nice but shoot for a simultaneous orgasm. By far the best sensation the human body can experience and it makes women more loyal to you (if they can feel such a thing).

62268369
>>62263653
>>62264007
Eh, it's still hot though. You get bored after 10 years of fucking 1 woman.

62268422
>>62259968
>FIRE
>implying it's the end
There's no reason you can't still invest. In fact, you should considering how many unexpected expenses can pop up unless you're filthy rich once you're 'done' with FIRE.

62268475
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
>never marry
>downside

62268693
>>62259935
It's kind of odd to me that people with such a net worth only ever consider retiring to do nothing.
A more natural progression seems to be managing your own wealth. When your net worth is so high you can only grow it by 5-10% per year, improving your portfolio's performance by a few % (not only from growth but also downside protection/risk management) has a huge impact.
Many probably buy into the "nothing beats buying and holding the index forever" narrative, and many don't want to bother dealing with finance things, but at some wealth:salary ratio it has to become unreasonable to avoid it.

62268709
>>62265928
the majority of porn/internet addicted retards don't even know this is possible. You have to be in tune together for this to happen and that's not possible if you're of a low vibration

62269694
>>62268709
It's pretty easy if you let her use a wand or clit sucking vibrator to give herself orgasms. The only tricky part is YOU cumming during one of her 5 or 8 orgasms.

62269751
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing
Why not? Are you a woman? It's usually only parasites annoyed by this.

62269890
>incel bait (((mysteriously))) ruins another anti waging thread
this reddit poster is obviously some low test gooner soiboi. In reality 1.9m in an index fund can get you around 150k a year which is more than enough for a non-incel life especially with a paid off house.

62269901
>>62259935
I'm 37 and once I hit a 2m net worth in the next couple of years I'm going to "retire" and go back into academia. It doesn't pay very well but at least the work is enjoyable and I get to spend time with other scientists instead of these bullshit artists in industry.

62269979
>>62259935
No women? The entire point of FIRE is to fuck wives while the husband works...did you even read the manifesto?

62270348
>>62259935
I'm 40 with only ~$400k net worth. No kids/wife/never been on a date. $25k annual spend.
Just get me a rope...

62270366
How dare you??
You are just a damn child.

62270374
>>62270348
I had $400k net worth when I was 40. I'm 44 now with $1.7 million net worth. A lot can change in a short amount of time.

62270394
>>62270374
>A lot can change in a short amount of time.
Bitcoin's growth is slower now and that's my only hope.
My salary is currently $100k and I don't see potential for any significant raises in my future.
At least I think I'll have the balls to exit the system at $1M and maybe find a part time job stocking shelves to keep me busy.

62270406
>>62270348
You're not missing anything. Society is fucked. I didn't start dating until I was ~33. One woman that I dated when I was 34 ruined me forever. Not financially - just mentally.

I'm worse off financially than you because of student loans but in a few more years, I'll be in better shape albeit still with like half of your net worth when I hit your age. You're in okay shape. You're doing much better than most of your peers. You can get the validation from a woman if you push for it but it won't help. I got validation from other women after the crazy one and I didn't feel a thing. Hopefully you have nieces and nephews for whom you can leave something behind. That's my plan even though I'll probably never even meet my nieces or nephews. So it goes.

62270414
>>62270406
Thanks, bro. I've got my music, I guess.
>nieces and nephews
Nah. I'm a single child raised by a single mother. Once she's gone, I'm all alone.

62270422
>>62270414
Sorry man. My nieces and nephews are the children of my cousins, not any siblings, and I don't have a relationship with my cousins. For all intents and purposes, I'll be pretty alone when my parents are gone and they're both up there in age.

Music is my thing too. I don't enjoy it as much anymore though. Nothing hits the same after that woman. You still have a lot of time to find someone especially since you have your shit together financially but just be careful. I don't want anyone to ever have to feel the way I do on account of a woman.

62270444
>>62270422
I hope things will work out for us in the end.
I'd definitely rather be in a band (something like Jaga Jazzist) than a relationship :) We'll see were things go.

62270945
>>62262891

OnlyFans is always hiring: no experience necessary, work from home at your own hours, no dress code required

62271089
>>62259968
so dont marry. i literally do not understand why you would if you have money and a house?
horny? hire a prostitute.
lonely? get friends, or see above.
existential crisis? realize its fake and gay, we all go into the ground eventually. dead and rotting in the ground.

62271100
>>62270366
i thought the chinese have a huge surplus of men
my mom got pregnant when she was 40 and had me. i only have a touch of 'tism

62271108
>>62268693
All the FIREs are also all bogles. They do not have the brains or cojones to try to get a +3%/year outperformance on the SPY (which I get easily in one of my bad but green months). They do not trust themselves that grand feat.

62271141
>>62263178
